title: Lidar system and method of operation
abstract: A lidar system, preferably including one or more transmit modules, beam directors, and/ or receive modules, and optionally including one or more processing modules. A method of lidar system operation, preferably including: emitting light beams, receiving reflected light beams, and/or analyzing data associated with the received light beams.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1. A method for environment mapping, comprising: â¢ at a lidar emitter, emitting a set of optical beams toward a beam director; â¢ at the beam director, directing each optical beam of the set outward of a sweep axis, wherein: â¢ the set of optical beams intersect a reference cylindrical surface at a set of points, wherein each optical beam of the set of optical beams intersects the reference cylindrical surface at a different point of the set of points; â¢ the reference cylindrical surface is coaxial with the sweep axis; and â¢ a plurality of points of the set of points are substantially arrayed along an array direction, wherein a reference line parallel to the sweep axis intersects the array direction at a substantially oblique angle; â¢ at the beam director, while directing each optical beam of the set outward of the sweep axis, sweeping each optical beam of the set about the sweep axis, wherein each optical beam of the set sweeps out a respective region of a respective right conical surface coaxial with the sweep axis; â¢ at a lidar receiver, receiving a plurality of reflected optical beams, each reflected optical beam originating from a reflection, off a respective object, of a different optical beam of the set of optical beams; and â¢ based on the plurality of reflected optical beams, determining, for each reflected optical beam of the plurality, a relative location of the respective object. 2. The method of Claim l, wherein a second plurality of points of the set of points are substantially arrayed along a second array direction perpendicular to the array direction. 3. The method of Claim 2, wherein the set of points substantially defines a rectangular array. 4. The method of Claim 1, wherein sweeping each optical beam of the set about the sweep axis comprises sweeping each optical beam about the sweep axis by a full rotation. 5. The method of Claim 1, wherein: â¢ the substantially oblique angle is less than 45 0 ; â¢ the set of optical beams defines a set of circles, wherein, for each optical beam, a respective intersection of the reference cylindrical surface and the respective right conical surface defines a respective circle of the set of circles, the respective circle associated with the optical beam; â¢ a largest spacing between two adjacent circles of the set defines a sweep spacing, wherein the two adjacent circles are associated with a first optical beam and a second optical beam; and â¢ a distance, between a first point of the set and a second point of the set, along the array direction, is greater than the sweep spacing, wherein the first point is associated with the first optical beam and the second point is associated with the second optical beam. 6. The method of Claim 5, wherein the distance is greater than twice the MSES. 7. The method of Claim 5, wherein the substantially oblique angle is between iÂ° and 15Â°Â· 8. The method of Claim 5, wherein: â¢ the lidar receiver comprises a first photodetector and a second photodetector; â¢ a first reflected optical beam, associated with the first optical beam, is received at the first photodetector; and â¢ a second reflected optical beam, associated with the second optical beam, is received at the second photodetector. 9 The method of Claim 5, wherein: â¢ the lidar emitter comprises a first laser and a second laser; â¢ the first optical beam is emitted by the first laser; and â¢ the second optical beam is emitted by the second laser. 10. The method of Claim 9, wherein: â¢ a first reflected optical beam, associated with the first optical beam, is received at a photodetector of the lidar receiver; and â¢ a second reflected optical beam, associated with the second optical beam, is received at the photodetector. 11. The method of Claim 10, wherein: â¢ the first optical beam encodes a first code; â¢ the second optical beam encodes a second code; and â¢ determining the relative locations of the respective objects of the first and second reflected optical beams comprises disambiguating the first and second reflected optical beams based on the first and second codes. 12. The method of Claim 1, wherein: â¢ the set of points substantially defines a hexagonal array, the hexagonal array defining the array direction, a second array direction, and a third array direction; â¢ the array direction, the second array direction, and the third array direction are coplanar and each intersect at 6oÂ° angles; â¢ the reference line intersects the second array direction at a second substantially oblique angle; and â¢ the reference line intersects the third array direction at a third substantially oblique angle. 13. The method of Claim 1, wherein each optical beam of the set of optical beams is emitted substantially concurrently. 14. The method of Claim 13, wherein each optical beam of the set of optical beams is emitted substantially continuously, wherein the lidar receiver receives the plurality of reflected optical beams while the lidar emitter emits the set of optical beams. 15. The method of Claim 1, wherein the beam director directs each reflected optical beam of the plurality to the lidar receiver. 16. A method for environment mapping, comprising: â¢ at a first laser of a lidar emitter, emitting a first plurality of beams; â¢ at a second laser of the lidar emitter, emitting a second plurality of beams, wherein: â¢ the first and second pluralities of beams cooperatively define an array of pixels; â¢ each pixel of the array comprises a beam of the first plurality and a beam of the second plurality; and â¢ the array of pixels defines an array direction, wherein a plurality of pixels of the array are arrayed along the array direction; â¢ at a beam director, directing each beam of the array of pixels outward of a sweep axis, wherein a reference line parallel to the sweep axis intersects the array direction at a substantially oblique angle; â¢ at the beam director, while directing each beam of the array of pixels outward of the sweep axis, sweeping each beam of the array of pixels about the sweep axis; â¢ at a lidar receiver, receiving a plurality of reflected beams, each reflected beam originating from a reflection, off a respective object, of a different beam of the array of pixels; and â¢ based on the plurality of reflected beams, determining, for each reflected beam of the plurality, a relative location of the respective object. 17. The method of Claim 16, wherein the array of pixels is a substantially hexagonal array, wherein the substantially oblique angle is substantially different from 30Â° and is substantially different from 6oÂ°. 18. The method of Claim 16, wherein: â¢ the substantially oblique angle is less than 45 0 ; â¢ the array of pixels comprises: â¢ a first pixel comprising a first beam of the array; and â¢ a second pixel comprising a second beam of the array; â¢ the array defines an angular spacing between the first and second beams, wherein a component, along the array direction, of the angular spacing defines a pixel gap; â¢ a minimum single emitter separation (MSES) between the first and second beams is less than the pixel gap, wherein the MSES is equal to a difference between a first polar angle, defined between the first beam and the sweep axis, and a second polar angle, defined between the second beam and the sweep axis. 19. The method of Claim 18, wherein receiving the plurality of reflected beams comprises: â¢ at a first photodiode of the lidar receiver, receiving each reflected beam of the first pixel; and â¢ at a second photodiode of the lidar receiver, receiving each reflected beam of the second pixel. 20. The method of Claim 19, wherein: â¢ each beam of the first and second pluralities is a substantially continuous-wave beam; â¢ each reflected beam of the first pixel is received at the first photodiode substantially concurrently; 

 â¢ each reflected beam of the second pixel is received at the second photodiode substantially concurrently; and â¢ the first and second pluralities of beams are emitted substantially throughout receiving the plurality of reflected beams. 21. A lidar system comprising an emitter, a beam director, and a receiver, wherein the lidar system is configured to perform the method of any of the preceding claims.
